title: Massive fire breaks out at hospital in Bihar's Muzaffarpur; several feared dead
content: Tragedy struck Prasad Hospital in Muzaffarpur, Bihar, as a devastating fire engulfed the ICU on Thursday. District Magistrate Subrat Kumar Sen confirmed that several lives were lost in the blaze. Firefighting teams responded swiftly, bringing the inferno under control. Initial reports indicate three fatalities, with more details anticipated as the situation unfolds.

title: Ropes, mattresses, bricks, bare hands: How locals fought to save guests in South Delhi hotel fire
content: Local heroes emerged as a fire engulfed a New Delhi bed and breakfast. Traders, neighbours, and labourers bravely used bricks, ropes, and mattresses to rescue trapped occupants. They smashed windows, created escape routes from rooftops, and entered the burning building to carry out unconscious victims, demonstrating extraordinary courage and community spirit.

title: Israel, Lebanon agree to renew ceasefire; create Lebanese security zones
content: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to renew a ceasefire and establish security zones in Lebanon, barring Hezbollah from these areas. This US-mediated deal hinges on a complete halt to Hezbollah fire and their withdrawal south of the Litani River, with the Lebanese army taking control.

title: 'I always try to stay one step ahead': Krunal Pandya
content: Fresh off back-to-back IPL titles, Krunal Pandya reflects on his journey, emphasizing resilience and reinvention. He discusses his evolution as a bowler in the high-scoring T20 era, the influence of his late father, and his unwavering hunger for success. Pandya also shares insights on his son's unique cricket critiques and his hopes for an India recall.

title: Trump’s 12.5% additional tariffs move under Section 301: What it means for India & trade deal talks?
content: The Section 301 probe launched by the US in March 2026 is a known variable in India’s trade deal talks with America. Yet, the proposal to impose duties on around 60 countries assumes significance at a time when a delegation from the US is in India to finalise terms of the India-US trade deal.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Meta tells 7,000 employees: You can make the real impact on this team
content: Meta is making big changes. Thousands of employees are moving to new AI teams. This is part of a major company-wide AI focus. CEO Mark Zuckerberg wants to strengthen Meta's AI position. Some workers are calling it an 'AI draft'. The company is also developing new AI tools. This restructuring shows Meta's growing commitment to artificial intelligence.

title: 'I was a little bit perturbed': Trump confirms calling Bibi 'crazy' over Lebanon strikes
content: US President Donald Trump admitted calling Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu "f***ing crazy" due to frustration over Lebanon escalation, impacting Iran talks. Despite the heated exchange, Trump insists their strong working relationship continues, citing shared "wartime leader" pressures. He remains optimistic about resolving regional tensions soon.

title: Delhi hotel fire: Family gathered for ailing elder; 8 of them are dead
content: A family gathering to be with a gravely ill elder turned tragic when eight members died in a hotel fire. The Aggarwal family, who had traveled to be with 75-year-old Radheshyam, were among the victims. Relatives are devastated and have not yet informed the ailing patriarch of the catastrophe.

title: ‘No regrets’: Vijay Shankar on retirement, 2019 WC heartbreak and the ‘3D player’ tag
content: In an exclusive conversation with TimesofIndia.com following his retirement announcement, former India allrounder Vijay Shankar reflected on the emotional decision to step away, the injuries that altered his career, handling public criticism, his love for all-round cricket and why he still wants to continue playing overseas leagues.

title: Google plans to release 32 million Wolbachia-infected mosquitoes across Florida and California
content: Alphabet's Debug initiative is seeking EPA approval to release 32 million specially treated male mosquitoes in California and Florida. This innovative approach uses Wolbachia bacteria to sterilize wild Culex mosquitoes, aiming to significantly reduce the spread of diseases like West Nile virus. Advanced AI and robotics are enabling this large-scale effort, with trials planned over two years.

title: DKS is Karnataka captain, but Siddaramaiah’s XI dominate team
content: D.K. Shivakumar sworn in as Karnataka's 24th CM, with G. Parameshwara as Deputy CM. The new cabinet balances caste and regional interests, reflecting Siddaramaiah's influence. Early initiatives focus on youth welfare, including free student bus passes and a private job portal. The ceremony highlighted religious traditions and drew national Congress leaders.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Official Album Released: Full 18-Track Lineup Revealed
content: FIFA has unveiled the 18-track Official FIFA World Cup 2026 Album, the largest music project ever linked to the tournament. Featuring global stars such as LISA, Shakira, Future, Tyla, The Rolling Stones and Burna Boy, the album aims to unite fans through music and football. Several songs are already available, while many artists will perform during Countdown Concerts and opening ceremonies across Mexico, Canada and the United States. The project also forms part of FIFA’s wider entertainment strategy ahead of the first-ever World Cup final halftime show.

title: US reaches EB-2 visa limit for Indians; new visas resume in October
content: Indian applicants for the EB-2 visa category will face a temporary halt as the US has exhausted its FY2026 quota. Embassies can no longer issue these visas until October 1, 2026, when the new fiscal year begins and limits reset. This impacts those seeking permanent residency through advanced degrees or exceptional abilities.

title: 'Wishing he was in high school': Cummins' hilarious Sooryavanshi admission
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi electrified IPL 2026 with a stunning 776 runs at a 230+ strike rate.  His fearless batting dismantled top bowlers, including Australia captain Pat Cummins.  Cummins admitted the teenager's power and confidence made him incredibly tough to bowl to, calling his ball-striking ability exceptional, regardless of age.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Chiefs eye Lions TE as Travis Kelce successor
content: The Kansas City Chiefs have been linked to a hypothetical trade for Detroit Lions tight end Sam LaPorta as a potential successor to Travis Kelce. The proposed deal would send a first- and third-round pick to Detroit for the 25-year-old Pro Bowler, who is entering the final year of his rookie contract. While LaPorta's recent injury history raises questions, his talent and long-term upside make him an intriguing target for Kansas City.

title: Maria Sharapova steals spotlight with stunning French Open return
content: Maria Sharapova made a surprise appearance at the 2025 French Open, delighting fans at Roland Garros. The five-time Grand Slam champion and two-time French Open winner posed for photos, met former star Gustavo Kuerten, and received praise across social media. Though retired since 2020, Sharapova remains one of tennis’ most popular figures and continues to attract worldwide attention.

title: Why TMC revolt is about Abhishek Banerjee, not Mamata
content: A Trinamool Congress rebellion is deepening, with expelled leader Ritabrata Banerjee challenging Abhishek Banerjee's authority rather than Mamata Banerjee's leadership. This internal strife, fueled by disputes over decision-making and the leader of opposition appointment, signals a proxy battle over succession, echoing past regional party struggles. The focus is on Abhishek's growing influence and organizational control.

title: Taylor Swift’s surprise wedding invite shocks fans, Blake Lively left out
content: Taylor Swift's upcoming wedding guest list is causing a stir. Karlie Kloss, with whom Swift had a public falling out, has received an invitation. However, Blake Lively, a former close friend, has not been invited. This decision stems from Lively's legal issues that exposed Swift's private information. Swift is prioritizing a drama-free event, showcasing her careful selection of confidantes.

title: Winds of change in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu may steady NDA's ship in Parliament
content: The BJP-led NDA sees a smoother legislative path as key opposition rivals TMC and DMK face internal challenges. A split within TMC and DMK's strained ties with Congress create opportunities for the ruling coalition to advance its agenda, including simultaneous elections, with renewed vigor.

title: Fixing in IPL? Lalit Modi says 'they're fixing in cricket', but not sure about IPL
content: Former IPL commissioner Lalit Modi has claimed that match-fixing persists in cricket, though he expressed uncertainty about its presence in the IPL due to the tournament's immense financial stakes. Modi suggested that smaller, less regulated domestic and regional leagues are more susceptible to manipulation and corruption, highlighting the massive betting market in cricket.

title: Cabinet backs ordinance to ease tax rules for foreign investors in some securities
content: The Union Cabinet has recommended an ordinance to ease tax rules for foreign investors in certain securities, aiming to strengthen the rupee. This move follows significant foreign portfolio investor withdrawals and addresses concerns raised by FPIs regarding capital gains and securities transaction taxes.

title: Iran targets US military vessel's 'command center' in Gulf of Oman, Centcom dismisses claim
content: Iran's state media reported its navy targeted a US destroyer in the Gulf of Oman, citing US actions against Iranian commercial vessels. The US Central Command refuted the claim, stating its military assets at sea are operating safely and unimpeded.

title: If TMC splits, speaker's role key in recognising 'real party'
content: Trinamool Congress faces a significant crisis with a faction claiming majority MLA support. The West Bengal assembly speaker's role is crucial in identifying the "original party," a decision guided by the Supreme Court's directives on party constitution and leadership structure outside the assembly, not just legislative numbers.

title: US moots 12.5% tariff on India, 53 others over ‘forced labour’
content: The US Trade Representative has proposed a 12.5% tariff on exports from 54 countries, including India and China, citing their failure to prohibit goods made with forced labor. This move comes as US officials are in India discussing a framework agreement. The USTR's report also addresses excess capacity in several countries, potentially leading to further tariffs.

title: CJP: Have no political links, fight bigger than any party
content: Ahead of a June 6 protest, the satirical platform Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) appointed three spokespersons to address public anger over examination controversies. The group, demanding the Education Minister's resignation, dismissed claims of being proxies for political opponents, emphasizing their fight is larger than individual affiliations.

title: Speculation mounts over TMC’s MPs, its heft in INDIA bloc
content: The Trinamool Congress faces a significant internal crisis in West Bengal, raising questions about its national parliamentary strength and its role within the INDIA bloc.  With a crucial opposition meeting scheduled, Mamata Banerjee's party is strategizing to maintain unity among its MPs and counter the BJP's influence amidst these developments.

title: Public sector bank accounts not needed for re-evaluation fee: CBSE
content: CBSE has clarified that students applying for Class XII answer sheet verification and re-evaluation can use various online payment options, not just specific bank accounts. The board also confirmed its online portal is functioning smoothly despite a significant cyberattack attempt shortly after its launch. Over 43,000 applications have been processed so far.

title: On June 10, Modi will break Nehru’s record of longest elected PM stint
content: Narendra Modi is set to become the longest-serving unbroken head of government in India, surpassing Jawaharlal Nehru on June 10. Modi's tenure, beginning May 26, 2014, will reach 4,399 days, just ahead of Nehru's 4,398 days. This milestone highlights a significant shift in Indian political leadership and policy direction.

title: Southampton erupts over police response to fatal stabbing in kirpan killer case
content: Southampton witnessed violent protests after police released bodycam footage of officers handcuffing a fatally wounded student, Henry Nowak, before his death from a kirpan attack. Demonstrators, alleging two-tier policing, clashed with riot police, causing injuries and damage. The incident has prompted a review of UK's anti-racism guidance.

title: After PM Modi’s nudge, mantris carpool to reach cabinet meet at Seva Teerth
content: Several Union ministers carpooled to a cabinet meeting at Seva Teerth. This follows Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call to reduce vehicle fleets. Ministers like Nitin Gadkari, J P Nadda, Ashwini Vaishnaw, and Mansukh Mandaviya shared rides. This initiative emphasizes sustainability and leads by example amid global energy concerns. Earlier, ministers and chief ministers had reduced their convoys.

title: Big fleet expansion: Navy to add 4 made-in-India warships, one survey vessel this month
content: The Indian Navy is set to significantly expand its fleet this month by inducting five indigenously-built naval platforms. These include two Project 17A stealth frigates, a survey vessel, and two anti-submarine warfare crafts, enhancing maritime security and reducing foreign dependence. The new additions bolster India's growing indigenous shipbuilding capabilities and combat readiness.

title: Congress to cadre: Ignore CJP, focus on NEET-CBSE stir
content: Congress plans to intensify its agitation on NEET-CBSE controversies over the next fortnight, urging members to focus on student and youth issues. The party is wary of rival political parties using the protests for their own agenda, drawing parallels to past movements. Youth Congress and NSUI are holding nationwide protests, emphasizing NEET as their primary concern.

title: SC collegium recommends 10 new judges for three HCs
content: The Supreme Court collegium, led by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, has recommended ten advocates and judicial officers for appointment as judges to the High Courts of Karnataka, Himachal Pradesh, and Madhya Pradesh. The recommendations include six advocates for the Karnataka HC, three judicial officers for the Himachal Pradesh HC, and one advocate for the Madhya Pradesh HC.

title: National Conference’s ‘secretive’ Dachigam huddle ends with Delhi protest plan for J&K statehood
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah convened a strategy session with National Conference MLAs and MPs at Dachigam National Park. The meeting concluded with a call for a protest in New Delhi during Parliament's monsoon session, demanding the restoration of J&K's statehood. The party also resolved to push for a ban on liquor in the region.

title: Can bar overqualified person from job: SC
content: The Supreme Court ruled that individuals with qualifications exceeding the maximum prescribed for a job can be disqualified. This decision aims to ensure employment opportunities for those with lower educational attainment, preventing overqualified candidates from displacing genuinely eligible applicants.

title: On Indo-China border, model village to come up in Ladakh for nomads
content: Ladakh's first model border village is being established at Chumur, a remote settlement at 16,700 feet along the India-China border. This initiative under the Vibrant Village Program will provide climate-resilient housing, enhance livelihoods through goat rearing and greenhouse cultivation, and integrate essential services to foster secure, permanent settlements and strengthen civil-defence cooperation.

title: From TMC insider to rebel leader: Who is Ritabrata Banerjee, man at the centre of turmoil
content: Expelled TMC leader Ritabrata Banerjee claims Speaker recognized him as opposition leader, signaling a major internal crisis. Fifty-eight rebel MLAs support this move, proposing a new leadership structure. This revolt, the party's biggest challenge, follows a significant election defeat and has prompted a TMC organizational overhaul.

title: Doctor reveals 4 health markers that may predict heart attack and stroke risk
content: Doctors are now suggesting looking beyond just LDL cholesterol for heart health. New markers like ApoB, hs-CRP, insulin resistance, and abdominal obesity provide a better and fuller picture. These factors reveal how inflammation, metabolism, and body fat impact vascular health. Understanding these elements helps identify risks earlier for better prevention.

title: Delhi fire tragedy: What India's fire safety rules actually require
content: A devastating hotel fire in Delhi claimed 21 lives, highlighting India's lax fire safety adherence. The National Building Code mandates stringent measures for building design, materials, and escape routes, especially for high-rises.  Strict enforcement and regular drills are crucial to prevent future tragedies, as the code's effectiveness hinges on implementation.

title: IRCTC flags 9 crore user accounts, expands AI monitoring across railway kitchens
content: IRCTC has taken decisive action against ticket booking fraud, deactivating over three crore suspicious user IDs and verifying six crore more. Simultaneously, an AI-powered kitchen monitoring system, utilizing 2,394 cameras, now oversees 800 kitchens, detecting nine types of hygiene issues to ensure food quality for millions of passengers.

title: ED summons TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee in teacher recruitment scam; asked to appear on June 15
content: Trinamool Congress MP Abhishek Banerjee has been summoned by the Enforcement Directorate to appear on June 15. The agency is investigating alleged irregularities and money laundering in the recruitment of primary teachers in West Bengal. Banerjee is expected to join the ongoing probe into the scam.

title: Planning to get your first loan or credit card? How lenders evaluate new-to-credit customers
content: Not having any credit accounts is a big disadvantage when looking to access new credit. But what can help is your existing relationship with banks, particularly those that hold your salary or savings account.

title: No place for third parties in border issues: MEA in response to Nepal PM while hosting country’s ruling party chief
content: MEA spokesperson responds to claims made by Balen Shah who had asserted that the boundary issue is a complex one with both India and Nepal having possession of each other’s territory and had sought intervention of the U.K. and China

title: Nepal is a priority partner, says Modi after meeting RSP chief
content: By focusing on shared civilizational bonds, digital corridors, and seamless connectivity, we can truly build a partnership defined by progress and mutual trust, says Rabi Lamichanne

title: Stage set for D.K. Shivakumar to take over as new CM of Karnataka
content: About 10 to 15 Ministers likely to be sworn in on Wednesday; no clarity on Deputy Chief Minister posts

title: D.K. Shivakumar sworn-in as 25th Chief Minister of Karnataka
content: Mr. Shivakumar, who took over the reigns of the State from veteran OBC leader Siddaramaiah, was sworn in along with 13 Cabinet ministers including Dr. G. Parameshwara.

title: Monsoon LIVE: Southwest monsoon expected to onset over Kerala around June 4, says IMD
content: One reason for the below-normal rainfall could be the emergence of El-Nino conditions, which lead to less rain during monsoon in the country

title: Delhi hotel fire: Locals turn saviours amid wails, screams, burning bodies
content: Rescuers say building’s single entry-exit point and lack of accessible windows made the rescue operation difficult

title: Remove restrictions on visitors to Secretariat: CPI(M)
content: A lot of restrictions are already in place. The new measures will prevent people from approaching the government machinery, says P. Shanmugam, State Secretary of the CPI(M), in a post on social media

title: Loyalists rally behind Mamata, say an ousted leader cannot lead Trinamool in Assembly
content: Earlier in the day, the Trinamool had dissolved all its party committees, including district and block units; party leaders say ‘Mamata is the original Trinamool Congress’

title: D.K. Shivakumar takes oath in the name of Ajjayya, and with the Constitution in hand
content: D.K. Shivakumar took oath in the name of the late seer Veera Gangadhara Ajjayya, his spiritual mentor, while holding the Constitution

title: 36 arrests in a day as Kochi City police launch ‘Operation Toofan’
content: Kochi City police registered 33 NDPS cases during raids across 90 locations as part of the Statewide anti-narcotics drive. Seven COTPA cases booked

title: ‘Assault’ on women: lookout notice to be issued against main accused
content: The main accused and six others are on the run, while the three arrested have been remanded in judicial custody

title: Thousands of teachers likely to lose jobs in Calicut varsity’s self-financing colleges
content: The Vice-Chancellor’s office is learnt to have issued orders to enforce UGC guidelines in their appointment. Clearance of UGC-NET or PhD is mandatory to become faculty members

title: An app to help eateries sell surplus food
content: The Kozhikode Corporation backs the initiative, citing its potential to divert food from landfills and reduce methane emissions

title: Unregulated quarrying upsets upland residents in Kozhikode
content: Residents allege quarrying and transportation of mined rock continue unabated despite repeated representations to local administrators and various government departments

title: Amid broken ties, Rahul, Kharge hail Karunanidhi on his birth anniversary
content: Move being seen as attempts to assuage DMK leadership, which is reportedly upset over the Congress’ tie-up with actor-turned politician Vijay-led Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam

title: NGT lacks jurisdiction to deal with coastal aquaculture units, rules Madras High Court
content: The High Court, says, now, it shall take suo motu notice of the issue of shrimp hatcheries located on the coasts of Tamil Nadu

title: Steps initiated for expanding fintech-based doorstep banking for Rajasthan villagers
content: A campaign titled ‘Sahakar Se Samriddhi’ (prosperity through cooperatives) has made new facilities available to the villagers for financial transactions and disbursal of loans.

title: Info panel rejects claim that CCTV footage in police station got auto-deleted
content: Says not preserving the data citing space constraints defeats the purpose of installing CCTV in police stations

title: Cross-border illegal arms, drug smuggling module busted in Amritsar
content: The recovered pistols include one .30 bore (made in China), two 9MM Glock pistols (made in Austria), one 9MM Glock pistol (made in USA) and two .30 bore pistols (made in Turkey).

title: U.S. proposes 12.5% tariff on India and other countries, Indian govt says it ‘remains engaged’ with U.S.
content: The action follows investigations launched against 60 countries over what the USTR described as their failure to impose and effectively enforce bans on imports made with forced labour

title: Karnataka CM swearing-in highlights: D.K. Shivakumar announces sops for "Yuva Yuga" in maiden briefing as chief minister
content: Earlier in the day, the 64-year-old Congress leader took oath in the name of late seer Gangadhar Ajjayya.

title: Bihar government to run 50 additional pink buses
content: 31 modern bus stands will be developed on the lines of the NOIDA, Mumbai bus stands. Intelligent Traffic Management System (ITMS) to be implemented under the PPP model to keep a tab on traffic violations.

title: Gujarat cabinet approves ‘wire-free city mission’; ₹500 crore allocated for first phase
content: Under the mission, all 17 municipal corporations and 151 municipalities in Gujarat will be covered in a phased manner.

title: Class VIII student dies after house roof collapses in Vellore
content: Parents and neighbours rushed the boy to Government Taluk Hospital in Gudiyatham where he succumbed to injuries

title: Residents welcome closure of Tasmac outlet in Tiruvannamalai
content: Residents celebrated the closure of the outlet, which they said caused public nuisance, by distributing sweets to neighbours and motorists

title: District administrations gear up for reopening of schools in Vellore, nearby districts
content: Many schools were given a facelift, like removal of bushes, erection of steel fences around the campus, replacing old electrical lines and water pipelines with new ones

title: TheraNym Biologics opens ₹1,200 crore manufacturing facility near Hyderabad
content: Telangana Industries Minister D. Sridhar Babu inaugurates the new facility of the Aurobindo Pharma subsidiary that is expected to generate job opportunities for more than 1,500 local youth

title: The Hindu Huddle 2026 — Art as a mirror to a world in transition
content: To be moderated by Narayan Lakshman, Opinion Editor of The Hindu and Curator of The Hindu Huddle, the session will feature artists Seema Kohli, Shilo Shiv Suleman, and art collector and founder of Bengaluru’s Museum of Art and Photography Abhishek Poddar

title: Russia delivers fourth squadron of AI-enabled S-400 air defence system
content: Three S-400 squadrons have already been inducted into service; the fourth has now arrived, following delays linked to the Russia-Ukraine conflict

title: Calls for bringing Attakulangara school campus alive
content: Improving the school’s standards and bringing its vast campus alive with children from the neighbourhood in the evenings and weekends are some of the suggestions to prevent further neglect

title: Explained | How D.K. Shivakumar finally became Karnataka’s Chief Minister
content: For decades, D. K. Shivakumar answered questions about becoming Chief Minister with a simple line: “Efforts may fail, but prayers do not.” Now, after a political journey spanning nearly four decades, he has finally reached Karnataka’s highest office.

title: Congress asks why PM protects Pradhan despite Education Ministry’s ‘incompetence and corruption’
content: The transfer of the CBSE’s top leadership has failed to address the issue of accountability at the highest levels of the Education Ministry, Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh says

title: Trinamool leader Jay Prakash Majumdar arrested for illegally occupying a house over year
content: Jay Prakash Majumdar, the state vice president of Trinamool Congress, joined the party in 2022 after he was suspended from BJP for publicly criticising the party for their defeat in 2021 assembly election.

title: Three arrested for attack on ‘Khan Sir’s’ coaching institute in Patna
content: Bihar Education Minister Mithilesh Tiwari says State government would formulate a policy within next three months to prevent rivalry among coaching institutes from disturbing law and order

title: Iran Says "No Tangible Progress" In Talks, Trump Signals Weekend Deal
content: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday that talks with Iran could yield results over the coming weekend and are going "very well."

title: US-Iran Live Updates: US House Backs Resolution To Halt Iran War, Defying Trump
content: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

title: Delhi Deadly Fire: From Uphaar To Malviya Nagar, Lessons Still Unlearnt
content: Delhi Fire: Twenty-one people died in a devastating fire at a bed-and-breakfast hotel in Delhi's Malviya Nagar on Wednesday, adding yet another chapter to the Capital's long and tragic history of deadly fire disasters.

title: Indian Man, 33, Charged In US Crash That Killed His Pregnant Teen Partner
content: Authorities said the accident occurred after Singh failed to stop for law enforcement and led deputies on a high-speed chase that reached speeds of about 100 miles per hour before ending in a collision.

title: Weather Live Updates: Southwest Monsoon Expected To Onset Over Kerala Today
content: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall over Kerala during the next week.

title: Did Lapses At Delhi's B&B Hotels Go Unchecked? Hauz Rani Fire Exposes Oversight Gaps
content: A fire tore through a hotel in Delhi's Malviya Nagar on Wednesday, killing at least 21 people, including 12 foreigners.

title: UK Lawmaker Sues Elon Musk's xAI Over Sexualised Grok Images
content: British lawmaker Jess Asato is suing Elon Musk's xAI, saying in a statement on Wednesday the Grok AI platform had been used to create fake sexualised images of her.

title: Iran Says It Targeted American Vessel In Sea Of Oman, US Dismisses Claim
content: According to a report by the Iranian news agency Tasnim, the vessel was struck as it neared Iran's territorial waters in the Sea of Oman.

title: "Epic Fury Is Over": Marco Rubio Says US No Longer Conducting Iran Strikes
content: The US-Israeli war, which Washington dubbed Operation Epic Fury, has engulfed the entire Middle East since the first strikes on Iran on February 28.

title: North Korea Unveils New Nuclear Plant, Vows "Exponential" Arsenal Boost
content: The official Korean Central News Agency said the facility used "more sophisticated technology" but didn't provide further details, such as where it is located and when it began its operation.

title: Trump Faces Setback As US House Passes Resolution To End Iran War
content: Iran War: The resolution, adopted with four members of Trump's Republican Party joining Democrats, was largely symbolic, as the US president can veto the measure if it gains Senate approval.

title: Despite US Diplomatic Efforts, Israeli Army Says "No Ceasefire" In Lebanon
content: The Israeli army's chief of staff, Eyal Zamir, has asserted that there is effectively no ceasefire in Lebanon, despite continuous efforts by US President Donald Trump to preserve the truce that initially came into effect in mid-April.

title: SpaceX Aims To Raise Record $75 Billion In Stock Market Debut: Report
content: SpaceX, the rocket and satellite company led by billionaire Elon Musk, aims to raise approximately $75 billion in a record initial public offering, the company said in a regulatory filing on Wednesday.

title: Trump Says He Will Attend G7 Summit In France
content: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday said in a post on Truth Social that he would travel to a Group of Seven leaders' summit in France.

title: Marco Rubio Calls India A Strategic Ally, Hopes For Quad Meeting This Year
content: In a written statement to the Committee, Rubio listed the US role in preventing a war between India and Pakistan among the "tremendous successes" of the US Department of State.

title: Gurugram Chartered Accountant, 7 Family Members Die In Delhi Hotel Fire
content: Vivek Agarwal, a chartered accountant from Sector 46 in Gurugram, had travelled to Delhi to visit his father, who is currently receiving treatment at Max Hospital in Delhi.

title: Shiv Sena's "Slow Poison" Barb Gets BJP's "Begani Shaadi..." Retort
content: In an interview with NDTV, Sattar claimed that the BJP's expansion within Maharashtra was coming at the cost of its alliance partners and described the process as a "slow poison" that was damaging the Shiv Sena's organisational base.

title: No longer conducting sustained strikes against Iran because 'Epic Fury' is over: Rubio
content: american secretary of state says the united states has ended its campaign of "sustained military strikes" in Iran . he said the operation had succeeded in destroying much of Iran's defence-industrial base . his remarks came as a fragile ceasefire continues to hold despite sporadic violence .

title: Experiment aborted too soon? How 'sidelined' Annamalai drifted away from BJP in Tamil Nadu
content: the former IPS officer has quit the BJP . he joined BJP in 2020 and made the party visible in the state like never before . sources close to Annamalai claim he has moved on and wants to put his own plans into action .

title: Flex-fuel vehicles practical solution to reduce crude imports: Hardeep Singh Puri
content:
NEW DELHI: Petroleum minister Hardeep Singh Puri on Wednesday said flex-fuel vehicles offer India a practical way to reduce crude oil imports. He said if even 1% of annual petrol vehicle sales shift to the technology, it would generate demand for four crore litres of ethanol and save Rs 195 crore in foreign exchange each year. Puri also said this would lead to a reduction of approximately 0.3 lakh metric tonnes in crude oil imports and net reduction of nearly 0.9 lakh metric tonnes of CO2 emissions. “Around Rs 160 crore would flow directly to our farmers instead of going out of the country for oil imports,” Puri said, adding that the impact would be significant as flex-fuel technology scales across India’s two-wheeler ecosystem. Transport minister Nitin Gadkari said India, as the world’s largest two-wheeler market, is well-positioned to lead the transition towards flex-fuel mobility and it would reduce emissions by 77%. tnn

End of Article
